calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For Order To Deposit Money Belonging To Minors Into Blocked Account
Off Calendar Aug-25-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carole Scagnetti for (petitioner) Daniel Matz.

The Court stated that this matter is on for an order to deposit money into blocked accounts rather than opening a guardianship and asked counsel if this is a new policy in the City's retirement system. Ms. Scagnetti reported that the city told them that this is their policy, that the decedent left a trust but these funds cannot go into the trust, that the petitioner is the minors father, that the petitioner has ample independent funds and does not intend to use these funds and that the trust provides funds for the minors education. The Court stated its concern that there will be no court oversight, acknowledged that it will not be a large amount of money when the minors reach majority, that it is inclined to grant the petition with the requirement that the funds are directly deposited into the blocked accounts, that receipts for opening the accounts are filed with the court and that there is not an automatic distribution at the age of majority. Ms. Scagnetti stated that funds have been accruing since December 2014 and that funds will be deposited on the 6th of each month. The Court granted the petition for order to deposit money belonging to minors into blocked accounts (subject to receipt/review of the order). Ms. Scagnetti is to submit a revised order with the requirements stated above.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
